info.title: Flowmanner API
info.description:
## Flowmanner API

Backend API for the Flowmanner workflow automation platform. Provides endpoints for:

- **Missions** -- Create, execute, and manage AI-powered workflow missions
- **Agents** -- Register and configure specialized AI agents
- **Chat** -- Real-time chat with AI models (WebSocket + REST)
- **Workspaces** -- Multi-user collaboration with role-based access
- **Graphs** -- Visual workflow builder and execution engine
- **Analytics** -- Usage metrics, token tracking, and cost analysis

### Authentication

All endpoints require a valid JWT token in the `Authorization` header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

Obtain tokens via `POST /api/v1/auth/login` or the OAuth flow.

### Rate Limiting

API requests are rate-limited per user. Limits are returned in response headers:
- `X-RateLimit-Limit` -- Maximum requests per window
- `X-RateLimit-Remaining` -- Remaining requests in current window
- `X-RateLimit-Reset` -- Unix timestamp when the window resets

get.description:
Lightweight liveness probe — returns cached component status.

Cache TTL is 5 seconds. For real-time deep diagnostics use
``GET /health/full``.
Deep diagnostic probe — runs all checks in real time (uncached).

Lightweight liveness probe — returns cached component status.

Cache TTL is 5 seconds. For real-time deep diagnostics use
``GET /health/full``.
Deep diagnostic probe — runs all checks in real time (uncached).

get.description:
Initiate OIDC login flow with PKCE.

Generates the authorization URL with PKCE challenge and returns it along with
state/nonce. The frontend should redirect the user to the authorization_url.

Query Parameters:
- redirect_uri: Optional custom redirect URI for after authentication

Handle OIDC callback after authentication.

This endpoint is called by the OIDC provider after the user authenticates.
It validates the state parameter, exchanges the authorization code for tokens,
and creates/finds the user.

Query Parameters:
- code: Authorization code from the OIDC provider
- state: State parameter for CSRF protection
- error: Error code if authentication failed
- error_description: Human-readable error description

post.description:
Exchange authorization code for tokens (API endpoint).

This is an alternative to the callback endpoint for SPAs that handle
the redirect client-side and want to exchange the code via API.

Request Body:
- code: Authorization code from the OIDC provider
- redirect_uri: The redirect URI used in the authorization request
- state: State parameter for CSRF protection

get.description:
Logout from OIDC provider.

Clears local tokens and returns the provider's end_session_url for redirect.

Query Parameters:
- id_token_hint: Optional ID token to include in logout request

post.description:
Refresh OIDC tokens.

Uses the stored refresh token to get new access/refresh tokens from the provider.

post.description:
Initiate subscription upgrade using PayPal.
Implements Story 3.3 (FR34) with PayPal instead of Stripe.

Handle PayPal webhook events for subscription lifecycle.

Processes:
  - BILLING.SUBSCRIPTION.ACTIVATED  → activate subscription, link to user
  - BILLING.SUBSCRIPTION.CANCELLED  → mark cancelled
  - BILLING.SUBSCRIPTION.SUSPENDED  → mark suspended
  - BILLING.SUBSCRIPTION.PAYMENT.FAILED → mark payment failed
  - BILLING.SUBSCRIPTION.EXPIRED    → mark expired

Expects the webhook ID to be set in PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID env var.

Activate a PayPal subscription after the user has approved it.

This is called from the frontend after the PayPal approval redirect.
It verifies the subscription with PayPal, activates it, and links
the UserSubscription to the user.

get.description:
Billing dashboard — current plan, usage vs limits, next billing date.

Shows:
  - Current plan tier and limits
  - Today's and this month's mission usage vs limits
  - Active (concurrent) mission count
  - API key count
  - Grace period status (if subscription expired)
  - Billing period dates
List available models from user active BYOK keys.

post.description:
Emit a webhook event to all active subscriptions.

Phase 8.5: Dispatches via Celery task for reliable delivery with
HMAC-SHA256 signing, exponential backoff retries, and DLQ.

get.description:
Return the live preview info for a sandbox.

Proxies to sandboxd's ``GET /v1/sandboxes/{id}`` and extracts the
``preview`` sub-object.  The preview URL format is::

    s-<sandbox-id>-<port>.preview.<domain>

Each exposed port gets its own subdomain; Traefik routes based on
the ``Host`` header.

Traefik forward-auth endpoint for gating sandbox preview URLs.

Traefik calls this endpoint before proxying to a sandbox container.
Checks for a valid FlowManner session via:
  1. ``Authorization: Bearer <token>`` header
  2. ``fm_refresh_token`` httpOnly cookie

Returns 200 + ``X-Forwarded-User`` if authenticated, 401 if not.
Traefik denies the request on non-2xx responses.

head.description:
Traefik forward-auth endpoint for gating sandbox preview URLs.

Traefik calls this endpoint before proxying to a sandbox container.
Checks for a valid FlowManner session via:
  1. ``Authorization: Bearer <token>`` header
  2. ``fm_refresh_token`` httpOnly cookie

Returns 200 + ``X-Forwarded-User`` if authenticated, 401 if not.
Traefik denies the request on non-2xx responses.

post.description: Create an anonymous playground sandbox. No auth required. Rate-limited.

get.description: Get playground sandbox status by sandboxd container ID.

post.description: Claim an anonymous playground sandbox. Requires authentication.

Requires session token via query param for anonymous sandboxes,

resolved (not found, wrong status, forbidden) are skipped rather than
failing the entire batch.

and sends them with event type 'hitl_inbox'.

Use when a plugin is found to be malicious or critically buggy.

get.description: Get aggregated health report for all plugins. Admin-only.
Return all agent personalities across all domains.

Return a single agent personality by its id (domain/slug).

The *path* is expected to be ``<domain>/<slug>`` where *domain*
may use hyphens (``software-it``) matching the frontend's
``DOMAIN_LABELS`` keys.  The backend directory uses underscores
(``software_it``), so we normalise automatically.

post.description:

Uses hybrid BM25 + vector search with a hard cap of 5 results.
All results are scoped to the requesting user's workspace.

get.description:

Returns all episode records associated with the given mission,
scoped to the requesting user's workspace.

This is an admin/testing endpoint that exposes the depth policy

Used for replay audit — shows why shallow vs deep was chosen

delete.description: Soft-delete (archive) a program.  Idempotency REQUIRED.  Rate limit 15/min.

Trigger a program run.  Idempotency REQUIRED.  Rate limit 10/min.

``payload`` is optional — manual fires need no body, but webhook
replays / cron re-fires can pass ``trigger_payload``.

List runs for a program, newest first.

post.description:

Rate limit ``program:consolidate`` (5/min — LLM-heavy).  Body is
optional; ``limit`` defaults to 10.

get.description:

program has no consolidated brief yet.

patch.description:
Update ONLY the user-owned ``user_notes`` sub-key of the brief.

No idempotency required (cheap, column-level).  Rate limit
``program:update`` (30/min) — same bucket as PATCH for fairness.

schema.title: Response Recall Claims Api V2 Personal Memory Recall Post
Response Inspector Api V2 Personal Memory Inspector Get

delete.description:
Hard-delete a claim. Returns 204 on success, 404 envelope on not-found.

204 is the project convention for DELETE (per v2 AGENTS.md rule 7).
404 returns the v2 envelope so clients can distinguish "exists in
another workspace" from "genuinely missing" without leaking the
distinction.

get.description:
Return the audit-trail summary for a single claim.

Thin envelope-wrapping layer over
``MemoryCorrectionService.get_provenance()`` (D30-60, T29 service).
The service does the heavy lifting (workspace-isolated event fetch
+ stable ``events_by_type`` bucket map); the route just unwraps
the dataclass into a Pydantic response.

Privacy guardrail: cross-tenant claims return
``event_count: 0, *_at: None`` (NOT 404) so a malicious user can't
probe whether a claim exists in a different workspace. This mirrors
the design of ``PersonalMemoryService.list_for_user`` for the same
reason.

schema.title: Response Claim Provenance Api V2 Personal Memory Claims  Claim Id  Provenance Get

get.description:
Paginated list of critiques for the current user+workspace.

Filters are all optional. The route is the only place where
``page``/``per_page`` are converted to ``offset``/``limit`` for
the service layer (service uses limit/offset; route exposes
page/per_page for clients — the v2 envelope convention).

Get a single critique by id, scoped to (user_id, workspace_id).

Returns 404 with code ``CRITIQUE_NOT_FOUND`` if the id does not
exist OR if the row exists but belongs to another (user,
workspace) tuple — the workspace-isolation guardrail surfaces a
"not found" to the caller to avoid existence-disclosure.

post.description:
Register a new user account and create an initial session.

Returns:
    201: { data: SessionResponse, meta, error: null }
    409: Email or username conflict
    422: Password validation failure

Authenticate user and create a new session.

Returns:
    201: { data: SessionResponse, meta, error: null }
    401: Invalid credentials
    200: { data: { requires_2fa: true, temp_token: "..." } } — 2FA required
    423: Account locked

Complete 2FA verification after login challenge.

Returns:
    200: { data: SessionResponse } — session created
    401: Invalid 2FA code or expired temp token

Refresh an access token using the refresh token.

Refresh token is read from:
1. httpOnly cookie (primary — extracted by AuthCookieMiddleware)
2. Request body (fallback for non-browser clients)

Returns:
    200: { data: SessionResponse }
    401: Invalid/expired/revoked refresh token
    401: Token reuse detected (entire family revoked)

delete.description:
Revoke a specific session.

Returns:
    204: No content (success)
    404: Session not found or not owned by user

get.description:
List all API keys for the authenticated user.

The full key is NEVER returned in list endpoints — only key_prefix is shown.

Returns:
    200: { data: [ApiKeyListResponse], meta, error: null }

post.description:
Create a scoped API key.

The full key is returned ONCE in this response. It is not stored in plaintext —
only the SHA-256 hash and prefix are persisted.

Returns:
    201: { data: ApiKeyResponse }
    400: Invalid scopes

delete.description:
Revoke an API key.

Returns:
    204: No content (success)
    404: Key not found or not owned by user

``limit`` controls how many of the most-recent completed runs are fed to
the consolidation LLM. Bounded 1..50 to prevent runaway consolidation
runs.

webhook replays and cron re-fires do.

HttpIntegrationConfigCreate.description: Request body for creating an HTTP integration config.

HttpIntegrationConfigUpdate.title: HttpIntegrationConfigUpdate
HttpIntegrationConfigUpdate.description: Request body for updating an HTTP integration config.

LoginRequest.title: LoginRequest
LoginRequest.description:
POST /auth/sessions — create a new session (login).

When provider='credentials': password is required.
When provider='oidc': password is ignored (OAuth/SSO flow uses OIDCLoginRequest
for the authorization URL, and the callback handles token exchange server-side).
For the initial OIDC login, use POST /auth/oidc/{provider}/login instead.

MissionStatus.title: MissionStatus
MissionStatus.description:
Typed mission lifecycle states with validated transitions.

External (user-visible): draft, pending, queued, running, completed, approved, failed, paused, aborted
Internal (executor phases): planning, planned, executing
Deprecated alias: cancelled (mapped to aborted)

MissionTaskStatus.title: MissionTaskStatus
MissionTaskStatus.description: Typed task lifecycle states with validated transitions.

NotesUpdate.title: NotesUpdate
NotesUpdate.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /programs/{id}/notes``.

Column-level update: only the user-owned ``user_notes`` sub-key of the
learning brief is touched.  Consolidation MUST NEVER overwrite this
field (per plan §T2 — column-level UPDATE discipline in the service
layer; this schema is the contract).

OAuthAppCreate.title: OAuthAppCreate
OAuthAppCreate.description: Request body for registering a user-provided OAuth app.

OAuthAppUpdate.title: OAuthAppUpdate
OAuthAppUpdate.description: Request body for updating an OAuth app.

OAuthInitiateRequest.title: OAuthInitiateRequest
OAuthInitiateRequest.description: Request to start an OAuth authorization flow.

PersonalMemoryClaimUpdate.title: PersonalMemoryClaimUpdate
PersonalMemoryClaimUpdate.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /claims/{id}`` (PATCH semantics).

All fields Optional. Fields NOT in this schema (user_id,
workspace_id, id, claim_type, scope, source_type, created_at,
updated_at) cannot be changed via PATCH — ``extra="forbid"``
raises 422 if a client tries.

Note: ``claim_type`` / ``scope`` / ``source_type`` are
intentionally NOT editable via PATCH because changing a claim's
taxonomy would invalidate provenance. Re-create the claim if you
need to reclassify.

PersonalMemoryForgetRequest.title: PersonalMemoryForgetRequest
PersonalMemoryForgetRequest.description:
Request body for the explicit ``POST /forget`` endpoint.

``claim_id`` is the UUID of the claim to forget. ``hard=False`` (the
default) is a soft-delete (sets ``deleted_at``); ``hard=True`` removes
the row from the table.

PersonalMemoryRecallRequest.title: PersonalMemoryRecallRequest
PersonalMemoryRecallRequest.description:
Request body for ``POST /claims/recall``.

``query`` is matched against (subject, predicate) via a simple
case-insensitive substring search in T19. T20+ will replace this
with semantic search via embeddings.

ProgramUpdate.title: ProgramUpdate
ProgramUpdate.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /programs/{id}`` — PATCH semantics.

All fields Optional. ``status`` is restricted to the documented literals
so a typo in the client returns 422, not 500.

RefreshSessionRequest.title: RefreshSessionRequest
RefreshSessionRequest.description:
POST /auth/sessions/refresh — refresh access token.

Refresh token read from httpOnly cookie (primary) or request body (fallback
with ?token_response=body).

capped.title: Capped
capped.description: True if results were capped at k=5

RunCreate.title: RunCreate
RunCreate.description: Create a run from a blueprint.

SandboxPreviewResponse.title: SandboxPreviewResponse
SandboxPreviewResponse.description: Preview info for a running sandbox.

Scope.title: Scope
Scope.description:
Visibility scope of a claim.

* ``personal`` — owned by the user, never shared.
* ``workspace`` — shared with workspace members.
* ``program`` — shared with a mission program's participants.
* ``private`` — encrypted at rest, only readable by the owner.

SelectPlanCandidateRequest.title: SelectPlanCandidateRequest
SelectPlanCandidateRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/v2/missions/{id}/select-plan.

Lets a user pre-select a non-default candidate before execution.
The chosen plan_id must match a row in mission_plan_candidates
for the mission; otherwise 404.

Sensitivity.title: Sensitivity
Sensitivity.description: PII / redaction level. Drives downstream filtering and redaction.

message.title: Message
message.default: New backup codes generated. Old codes are now invalid.

message.title: Message
message.default: 2FA enabled successfully. Save your backup codes — they won't be shown again.
